description: Shared contract for all AI agents operating the Autopilot control plane

sessionGuard.description: Multi-agent concurrency control. Required when multiple agents share the same GitHub account.
protocol: Check if session-lock.json exists and is not expired
If locked by DIFFERENT agent, ABORT with error
If locked by SAME agent, refresh the lock
If expired or no lock, acquire new lock
Always release lock in the audit/cleanup stage

workspaceDiscovery.method: List directories under state/workspaces/ on autopilot-state branch
workspaceDiscovery.note: There is NO default workspace. Agents MUST identify the target workspace from conversation context before any operation.

workspaceIdentification.description: MANDATORY protocol for identifying which workspace to operate on. No workspace is default.
protocol: 1. BEFORE any operation, identify which company/workspace the user is talking about
2. Look for context clues: company name, repo names, stack mentions, tool mentions
3. If context is ambiguous, ASK the user which workspace before proceeding
4. NEVER assume a workspace — always confirm from context or ask
5. Once identified, verify workspace_id exists in state/workspaces/
6. Read workspace.json to get repos, token, and config for that workspace

rules: NEVER assume a default workspace — always identify from conversation context or ask the user
Never store corporate secrets in the autopilot repo
Never store corporate code in the autopilot repo
Always use workspace_id, never hardcode tenant/org names
Always acquire lock before writing release state
Always release lock after operation completes or fails
Always write audit entry after any state mutation
Never use regex to edit YAML - use structured tooling
Never hardcode branch names - read from workspace.json
State is the source of truth, not agent memory
Always validate jq output with fallbacks (2>/dev/null || echo fallback)
Use base64 encoding when passing content between workflow jobs
Never silently swallow errors - always log before continuing
Never mix data, credentials, or operations between workspaces

errorHandling.jqCalls: Always add 2>/dev/null || echo 'fallback' to jq commands
errorHandling.apiCalls: Log error details before continuing or failing
errorHandling.lockRelease: Log warning if lock release fails, never silent || true
errorHandling.auditWrite: Retry once if audit write fails, then log error
